decisions.decision: r(T) drops from 0.93 to 0.63 (T=400 to T=2.68e11); fit: 1.30-0.20*log(log T)
decisions.rationale: CLT effect (more RS terms -> more Gaussian), not mechanism weakening
Condition E[z_G z_A]<2r holds whenever r>0 because mean product ~ -0.2 to -0.6 << 2r

decisions.decision: r > 0 proved via spectral variance identity + sub-interval positivity + ergodic theorem
decisions.rationale: V(g)=2Var_p[cos(wg/2)]/(1+C(g))>=0 by def of variance; h(g)=sqrt(2V/pi) goes 0->0.80; Cov positive at every partition; Weyl gives fixed-phase convergence

decisions.decision: Influence function proxy FAILS at T~10^11 but actual delta_r still works
decisions.rationale: Phantom/peak ratio = 2.27 (>>1), but removing zeros still decreases r on average

next_action: Either: (1) make the proof journal-ready by proving V(g) core monotonicity analytically, or (2) submit the two papers as-is with the proof at sketch level, or (3) push the density bound to Odlyzko's T~1.44e20 zeros (need mpmath arbitrary precision for gap resolution).
context_notes: Sessions 11-12 transformed the project from 'strong numerical observation' to 'proof sketch with massive numerical verification'. The proof chain is: spectral variance identity (exact) -> V(g) > 0 (incommensurability) -> h(g) shape 0->0.80 -> sub-interval positivity (every partition gives Cov>0) -> Cov(g,P) > 0 -> r > 0 -> mean influence negative -> density bound. Verified at T=400 to T=2.68e11 (11 orders of magnitude). Two papers: rigidity_exclusion_v3.tex (density bound) and r_positivity_theorem.tex (proof of r>0). The venv is broken (OneDrive sync); use /c/Python313/python.exe directly.
